summary: Solid soundboard recording (needs slight pitch correction) captures a strong second set with classic Eyes of the World, perfect Truckin' reprise and tight Black Peter.
review: The show offers several standout moments—most notably a quintessential '74 Eyes of the World, an almost flawless second‑half Truckin', and a tight Black Peter—all while the first set sits at average level. The soundboard source is decent but suffers from variable speed/pitch issues that require a small slowdown to sound correct.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed review notes a good performance with multiple highlights, offset by pitch/speed problems in the recording; weighted assessment yields an overall four‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Pitch runs too fast and varies throughout; needs about -2% speed correction (some tracks -3%). Image is better than alternative Miller source but the pitch issue detracts from overall clarity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is average to above‑average, hampered by heat and a few off‑notes; the second set shines with rare moments (train‑whistle chords, pre‑hiatus Drums>Space) and tighter jams, earning an overall B‑ rating.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Shows rare train‑whistle chord work on Big Railroad and strong riff interplay throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's keyboard interplay is highlighted during Mexicali and other jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy stays on drums for a rare pre‑hiatus Drums>Space segment.
